properties.description: Quota properties for the specified resource, based on the API called, Quotas or Usages.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

unit.description: The quota units, such as Count and Bytes. When requesting quota, use the **unit** value returned in the GET response in the request body of your PUT operation.

name.description: Resource name provided by the resource provider. Use this property name when requesting quota.

resourceType.description: The name of the resource type. Optional field.

quotaPeriod.description:
The time period over which the quota usage values are summarized. For example:
*P1D (per one day)
*PT1M (per one minute)
*PT1S (per one second).
This parameter is optional because, for some resources like compute, the period is irrelevant.

isQuotaApplicable.description: States if quota can be requested for this resource.

properties.description: Additional properties for the specific resource provider.

limitType.description: The quota or usages limit types.

createdBy.description: The identity that created the resource.

createdByType.description: The type of identity that created the resource.

lastModifiedBy.description: The identity that last modified the resource.

lastModifiedByType.description: The type of identity that last modified the resource.

lastModifiedAt.description: The timestamp of resource last modification (UTC)

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

displayName.description: Display name of the GroupQuota entity.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

enforcementEnabled.description: Is the GroupQuota Enforcement enabled for the Azure region.

enforcedGroupName.description: The name of the group that is enforced.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

provisioningState.description: Status of this subscriptionId being associated with the GroupQuotasEntity.

etag.description: "If etag is provided in the response body, it may also be provided as a header per the normal etag convention.  Entity tags are used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource. HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.")

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

provisioningState.description: The status of the underlying ARM resource operation.

transferStatus.description: The business status of the transfer.

transferId.description:
Server-generated identifier the recipient uses to address the transfer on the
incomingQuotaTransfers URI.

displayName.description: Human-friendly label surfaced on customer GET responses and recipient inbox listings.

comment.description: Donor-supplied free-text rationale captured at submit time.

destinationSubscriptionId.description: Recipient subscription id. Must differ from the donor subscription.

destinationTenantId.description: Recipient tenant id, resolved by the service from the recipient subscription.

billingAccountId.description: Billing account id both donor and recipient subscriptions must roll up to.

resourceName.description:
The quota dimension being moved, scoped by the URI's target provider
(for example, `standardDv5Family` under Microsoft.Compute).

amount.description: Amount to transfer in the resource's native unit (e.g. vCPU count).

autoApprove.description:
Same-tenant one-shot opt-in. When true, the donor PUT admission-checks recipient-side
RBAC and cap at submit time and drives the transfer to terminal Completed within the
same LRO, with no recipient approve required. The outcome is reflected by
`transferStatus`: `Completed` means the auto path committed; `Pending` means it did
not (e.g. cross-tenant, missing RBAC, cap exceeded) and the recipient must approve.

expiresAt.description: Time at which a Pending transfer expires if the recipient has not approved or rejected it.

approval.description:
Approval record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Accepted` or `Completed`.
Mutually exclusive with `cancellation`.

cancellation.description:
Cancellation record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Cancelled`.
Mutually exclusive with `approval`.

comment.description: Optional free-text comment supplied by the approver.

actor.description: Principal that performed the approval (typically a UPN or service principal id).

occurredAt.description: Timestamp at which the approval was recorded.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason supplied by the donor when cancelling.

occurredAt.description: Timestamp at which the cancellation was recorded.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

error.description: Error details of the quota request.

requestSubmitTime.description: The quota request submission time. The date conforms to the following format specified by the ISO 8601 standard: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ

resourceType.description: Resource type for which the quota properties were requested.

unit.description: Quota limit units, such as Count and Bytes. When requesting quota, use the **unit** value returned in the GET response in the request body of your PUT operation.

properties.description: Usage properties for the specified resource.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

usages.description: The quota limit properties for this resource.

unit.description: The units for the quota usage, such as Count and Bytes. When requesting quota, use the **unit** value returned in the GET response in the request body of your PUT operation.

name.description: Resource name provided by the resource provider. Use this property name when requesting quota.

resourceType.description: The name of the resource type. Optional field.

quotaPeriod.description:
The time period for the summary of the quota usage values. For example:
*P1D (per one day)
*PT1M (per one minute)
*PT1S (per one second).
This parameter is optional because it is not relevant for all resources such as compute.

isQuotaApplicable.description: States if quota can be requested for this resource.

properties.description: Additional properties for the specific resource provider.

usagesType.description: The quota or usages limit types.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

requestSubmitTime.description: The request submission time. The date conforms to the following format specified by the ISO 8601 standard: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ

limit.description: The new quota limit for the subscription. The incremental quota will be allocated from pre-approved group quota.

name.description: Name of the resource provided by the resource provider. This property is already included in the request URI, so it is a readonly property returned in the response.

region.description: Location/Azure region for the quota requested for resource.

comments.description: GroupQuota Request comments and details for request. This is optional paramter to provide more details related to the requested resource.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

value.description: List of Group Quota Limit details.

nextLink.description: The URL to use for getting the next set of results.

properties.description: Group Quota properties for the specified resource.

resourceName.description: The resource name, such as SKU name.

limit.description: The current Group Quota Limit at the parentId level.

comment.description: Any comment related to quota request.

unit.description: The usages units, such as Count and Bytes. When requesting quota, use the **unit** value returned in the GET response in the request body of your PUT operation.

name.description: Name of the resource provided by the resource provider. This property is already included in the request URI, so it is a readonly property returned in the response.

availableLimit.description: The available Group Quota Limit at the MG level. This Group quota can be allocated to subscription(s).

value.description: List of Group Quota Limit allocated to subscriptions.

quotaAllocated.description: The amount of quota allocated to this subscriptionId from the GroupQuotasEntity.

provisioningState.description: Status of this subscriptionId being associated with the GroupQuotasEntity.

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

requestedResource.description: The new quota request allocated to subscription.

limit.description: The new quota limit for the subscription. The incremental quota will be allocated from pre-approved group quota.

nextLink.description: The URL to use for getting the next set of results.

properties.description: Quota properties for the specified resource.

resourceName.description: The resource name, such as SKU name.

limit.description: The total quota limit for the subscription.

shareableQuota.description: The shareable quota for the subscription.

name.description: Name of the resource provided by the resource provider. This property is already included in the request URI, so it is a readonly property returned in the response.

properties.description: Properties of the incoming quota transfer.

etag.description: "If etag is provided in the response body, it may also be provided as a header per the normal etag convention.  Entity tags are used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource. HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.")

systemData.description: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

provisioningState.description: The status of the underlying ARM resource operation.

transferStatus.description: The business status of the transfer.

transferId.description: Server-generated identifier of the transfer (matches the URI key).

transferRef.description: Fully qualified ARM resource id of the donor-side quotaTransfers resource.

sourceSubscriptionId.description: Donor subscription id. The recipient subscription is the one in the request URI.

sourceTenantId.description: Donor tenant id, resolved by the service from the donor subscription.

billingAccountId.description: Billing account id both donor and recipient subscriptions roll up to.

amount.description: Amount being transferred in the resource's native unit.

sourceEtag.description:
ETag of the donor-side source document at the time the inbox entry was projected. Used
as the If-Match value on approve and reject requests.

approval.description:
Approval record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Accepted` or `Completed`.
Mutually exclusive with `rejection`.

rejection.description:
Rejection record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Rejected`.
Mutually exclusive with `approval`.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason supplied by the recipient when rejecting.

occurredAt.description: Timestamp at which the rejection was recorded.

comment.description: Optional free-text comment recorded on the transfer.

properties.description: Properties of the incoming quota transfer.

etag.description: "If etag is provided in the response body, it may also be provided as a header per the normal etag convention.  Entity tags are used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource. HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.")

id.description: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. E.g. "/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}"

type.description: The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"
